info.title: WP Command Center REST API
info.description: REST API for managing WordPress sites — Site Intelligence, Diagnostics, File Access, Patch Engine, Operations, MCP, and Agent orchestration.

bearerAuth.description: WP Command Center API token created from Settings → API Tokens. Read-only tokens access GET routes; full-access tokens are required for POST/PATCH/DELETE write actions.

path.description: Path relative to wp-content/ (themes/, plugins/, or mu-plugins/)

get.summary: Health check for the API gateway

post.summary: Run read-only frontend, admin, REST, WPCC, WooCommerce, plugin, and theme health checks

get.summary: List persisted health verification results
Get the current WP Command Center environment mode

post.summary: Set environment mode
Dry-run or delete age-qualified terminal runtime records with environment-aware safeguards

confirm.description: Confirmation phrase required for live cleanup

get.summary: Machine-readable description of this API for agent discovery

200.description: Endpoint manifest with paths, methods, scopes, and descriptions

get.summary: Agent discovery manifest: capabilities, security posture, workflow, endpoint catalog, error catalog, capability negotiation, and version info
Composite context bundle: site info, diagnostics, server capabilities, and file access map
Metadata-only agent runtime context

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Limit context to a specific session

get.summary: Unified traceable timeline of the agent lifecycle
Hierarchical agent runtime tree (Session -> Task -> Action -> Plan -> Patch)
List agent sessions, newest first

post.summary: Create an agent session

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp. Defaults to 24 hours from creation.

get.summary: Get an agent session by UUID

post.summary: Close an active agent session

get.summary: List agent tasks, newest first

get.summary: Get an agent task by UUID

post.summary: Update an agent task status

get.summary: List agent actions, newest first

post.summary: Record an agent action (metadata only, does not execute)

get.summary: Get an agent action by UUID

post.summary: Accept a proposed action
Reject a proposed action
Cancel a proposed or accepted action
Mark an accepted action as completed

get.summary: List agent plans with ordered steps, newest first

post.summary: Create a plan under an existing session and task

get.summary: Get an agent plan and its ordered steps by UUID

post.summary: Approve a pending_review or draft plan

get.summary: List all patches (summary records)

post.summary: Create a patch proposal

201.description: Patch created with status=pending_approval and diff

get.summary: Get a single patch record including diff and status history

post.summary: Approve a pending patch
Apply an approved patch — auto-snapshots, writes, verifies, auto-reverts on failure

400.description: Status does not allow apply / file changed on disk

post.summary: Roll back an applied patch using its snapshot(s) with hash verification
Reject a pending or approved patch

get.summary: List all supported WordPress operations (metadata only)

200.description: List of operations with parameters, risk levels, and availability

get.summary: Get detailed metadata for a specific operation

post.summary: Execute the content seeding operation
Populate ACF fields on a post
Generate a Contact Form 7 form
Create a simple WooCommerce product
Perform a safe database search and replace
Import a remote image to the Media Library
Safely update a plugin or theme with health verification
Run a structured WP-CLI command
Safely inspect or update a registered WordPress option
Safely inspect and manage WordPress plugins
Safely inspect and manage WordPress themes
Create, list, inspect, verify, and restore file snapshots
Safely inspect and manage WordPress content
Read-only database health and structure inspection
Manage platform capabilities

get.summary: Get detailed metadata for a specific operation request

Execute an approved operation request
Queue an approved operation request for later execution

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status (queued, running, completed, failed, cancelled)
